messages.content:
I am getting an error 'Object Required'
on this line 'Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i) & wscf.Cells(i, "H"))'
in this code below.
For i = 5 To lastRow
    If wscf.Cells(i, "I") = "" Then
        If copyRange Is Nothing Then
            Set copyRange = wscf.Range("J" & i)
If wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value <> "" Then
    copyRange.Value = wscf.Range("J" & i) & wscf.Cells(i, "H")
End If
        Else
            Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i) & wscf.Cells(i, "H"))
            End If
        End If
Next i

The error "Object Required" occurs because you are trying to concatenate two range objects `wscf.Range("J" & i)` and `wscf.Cells(i, "H")` using the `&` operator. 

The `&` operator is used for concatenating strings, but in this case, you are trying to concatenate two range objects. To fix the error, you need to concatenate the values of the two range objects instead.

Replace the line:
```
Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i) & wscf.Cells(i, "H"))
```
with:
```
Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i).Value & wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value)
```

This will concatenate the values of the two range objects, which is what I assume you intended to do.
error object required on the modified line

Apologies for the confusion. I misunderstood your requirement.

To fix the "Object Required" error, you should set the copyRange as a Range object and use the Union method properly.

Here's the corrected line:

```
Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i), wscf.Cells(i, "H"))
```

Note that we need to separate the separate range objects by commas within the Union method.

Here's the updated code:

```VBA
Dim copyRange As Range
Dim i As Long
Dim lastRow As Long

lastRow = wscf.Cells(wscf.Rows.Count, "I").End(xlUp).Row

For i = 5 To lastRow
    If wscf.Cells(i, "I") = "" Then
        If copyRange Is Nothing Then
            Set copyRange = wscf.Range("J" & i)
        End If
        If wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value <> "" Then
            Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i), wscf.Cells(i, "H"))
        End If
    End If
Next i
```

Now, the `copyRange` will be a union of all the specified range objects.
it copied all the values in H and added it to the very first value of J. It did not copy the values of H to the corresponding row value of J

Apologies for the confusion. To copy the values from column H to the corresponding row in column J, you need to assign the value of column H to the corresponding cell in column J.

Here's the corrected code:

```VBA
Dim copyRange As Range
Dim i As Long
Dim lastRow As Long

lastRow = wscf.Cells(wscf.Rows.Count, "I").End(xlUp).Row

For i = 5 To lastRow
    If wscf.Cells(i, "I") = "" Then
        If copyRange Is Nothing Then
            Set copyRange = wscf.Range("J" & i)
        Else
            Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i))
        End If
        If wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value <> "" Then
            wscf.Cells(i, "J").Value = wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value
        End If
    End If
Next i
```

In this updated code, I have added the line `wscf.Cells(i, "J").Value = wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value` to copy the value in column H to the corresponding cell in column J for each non-empty cell in column H.

Please give it a try and let me know if you have any further questions!

If copyRange Is Nothing Then
            Set copyRange = wscf.Range("J" & i)
        End If
        If wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value <> "" Then
            Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i), wscf.Range("H" & i))
        End If
    End If
Next i

The Values in column "I" are listed as follows:
I5: 20/10/23
I6:
I7:
I8:
I9:
I10:

The Values in column "J" are listed as follows:
J5: Completed Tasks
J6: Shower Head descale
J7: Legionella Analysis
J8: Hot & Cold Water Temperature
J9: Water Tank Inspection
J10: Sports Building Water Tank

The Corresponding row values in "H" are listed as follows:
H5:
H6: 05/09/2023
H7: 09/12/2023
H8: 15/09/2023
H9: 28/08/2024
H10: 

When I run the code above, this is what I am expecting as the output:
D9: Shower Head descale 05/09/2023
D10: Legionella Analysis 09/12/2023
D11: Hot & Cold Water Temperature 15/09/2023
D12: Water Tank Inspection 28/08/2024
D13: Sports Building Water Tank

This is what the code above is outputting:
Shower Head descale05/09/202305/09/202305/09/2023
Legionella Analysis
Hot & Cold Water Temperature Checks
Water Tank Inspection

Apologies for the confusion. To achieve the desired output, you need to modify the code as follows:

```VBA
Dim copyRange As Range
Dim i As Long
Dim lastRow As Long

lastRow = wscf.Cells(wscf.Rows.Count, "I").End(xlUp).Row

For i = 5 To lastRow
    If wscf.Cells(i, "I").Value = "" Then
        If copyRange Is Nothing Then
            Set copyRange = wscf.Range("J" & i)
        Else
            Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i))
        End If
        If wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value <> "" Then
            copyRange.Offset(, 1).Value = wscf.Cells(i, "J").Value & " " & wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value
        End If
    End If
Next i
```

In this updated code, the value in column H is concatenated with the value in column J and then assigned to the adjacent cell in column K using the `Offset` property.

Please give it a try and let me know if you have any further questions!

With your code, this is what the output is:
Shower Head descale05/09/202305/09/202305/09/2023
Legionella Analysis
Hot & Cold Water Temperature Checks
Water Tank Inspection
Sports Building Water Tank Sediment Clean

This what the output should be:
D9: Shower Head descale 05/09/2023
D10: Legionella Analysis 09/12/2023
D11: Hot & Cold Water Temperature 15/09/2023
D12: Water Tank Inspection 28/08/2024
D13: Sports Building Water Tank

Apologies for the confusion. To achieve the desired output, please try the following modified code:

```VBA
Dim copyRange As Range
Dim i As Long
Dim lastRow As Long
Dim outputRow As Long

outputRow = 9 ' specify the starting row for the output

lastRow = wscf.Cells(wscf.Rows.Count, "I").End(xlUp).Row

For i = 5 To lastRow
    If wscf.Cells(i, "I").Value = "" Then
        If copyRange Is Nothing Then
            Set copyRange = wscf.Range("J" & i)
        Else
            Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i))
        End If
        If wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value <> "" Then
            wscf.Cells(outputRow, "D").Value = wscf.Cells(i, "J").Value & " " & wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value
            outputRow = outputRow + 1
        End If
    End If
Next i
```

In this updated code, I have introduced a new variable `outputRow`, which specifies the starting row for the output in column D. The concatenated value from columns J and H is now assigned to the corresponding row in column D, starting from the specified `outputRow`. The `outputRow` is incremented after each successful assignment.

Please give it a try and let me know if it works as expected!

This what the output should be:
Shower Head descale 05/09/2023
Legionella Analysis 09/12/2023
Hot & Cold Water Temperature 15/09/2023
Water Tank Inspection 28/08/2024
Sports Building Water Tank Sediment Clean.

Apologies for the confusion. To achieve the desired output, please try the following modified code:

```VBA
Dim copyRange As Range
Dim i As Long
Dim lastRow As Long

lastRow = wscf.Cells(wscf.Rows.Count, "I").End(xlUp).Row

For i = 5 To lastRow
    If wscf.Cells(i, "I").Value = "" Then
        If copyRange Is Nothing Then
            Set copyRange = wscf.Range("J" & i)
        Else
            Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i))
        End If
        If wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value <> "" Then
            copyRange.Offset(copyRange.Rows.Count - 1, 1).Value = wscf.Cells(i, "J").Value & " " & wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value
        End If
    End If
Next i
```

In this updated code, the value in column H is concatenated with the value in column J and then assigned to the adjacent cell in column K, which is determined by `copyRange.Rows.Count - 1`. This ensures that each concatenated value is placed on the last row of the copyRange.

Please give it a try and let me know if it works as expected!

With your code, this is what the output is:
Shower Head descale05/09/202305/09/202305/09/2023
Legionella Analysis
Hot & Cold Water Temperature Checks
Water Tank Inspection
Sports Building Water Tank Sediment Clean

It is duplicating the date to the first out put row and not distrubuting the dates as shown below.

This what the output should be:
Shower Head descale 05/09/2023
Legionella Analysis 09/12/2023
Hot & Cold Water Temperature 15/09/2023
Water Tank Inspection 28/08/2024
Sports Building Water Tank Sediment Clean

Apologies for the confusion. To distribute the dates correctly, we need to keep track of the number of non-empty cells in column H and adjust the offset accordingly.

Here's the modified code that should produce the desired output:

```VBA
Dim copyRange As Range
Dim i As Long
Dim lastRow As Long
Dim offsetCount As Long

lastRow = wscf.Cells(wscf.Rows.Count, "I").End(xlUp).Row
offsetCount = 0

For i = 5 To lastRow
    If wscf.Cells(i, "I").Value = "" Then
        If copyRange Is Nothing Then
            Set copyRange = wscf.Range("J" & i)
        Else
            Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i))
        End If
        If wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value <> "" Then
            offsetCount = offsetCount + 1
            copyRange.Cells(offsetCount, 2).Value = wscf.Cells(i, "J").Value & " " & wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value
        End If
    End If
Next i
```

In this updated code, the `offsetCount` variable keeps track of the number of non-empty cells in column H. We adjust the offset using `offsetCount` when assigning the concatenated value to the adjacent cell (column K) relative to `copyRange`. 

Please give it a try and let me know if it produces the desired outcome!
the dates are not being concantenated with the other J values. They are all duplicated with only the first value found in J

```VBA

lastRow = wscf.Cells(wscf.Rows.Count, "I").End(xlUp).Row
offsetCount = 0

For i = 5 To lastRow
    If wscf.Cells(i, "I").Value = "" Then
        If copyRange Is Nothing Then
            Set copyRange = wscf.Range("J" & i)
        Else
            Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i))
        End If
        If wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value <> "" Then
            offsetCount = offsetCount + 1
            copyRange.Cells(offsetCount, 2).Value = wscf.Cells(i, "J").Value & " " & wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value
        End If
    End If
Next i
```

In this updated code, we adjust the `offsetCount` variable each time a concatenated value is assigned to the adjacent cell.

Please give it a try and let me know if it produces the desired outcome!
No it is still doing the same thing. It is not cancateating the values correctly

Apologies for the confusion. To concatenate values correctly, we need to first convert the range values to strings before concatenating them. Here's the modified code:

```VBA
Dim copyRange As Range
Dim i As Long
Dim lastRow As Long
Dim offsetCount As Long

lastRow = wscf.Cells(wscf.Rows.Count, "I").End(xlUp).Row
offsetCount = 0

For i = 5 To lastRow
    If wscf.Cells(i, "I").Value = "" Then
        If copyRange Is Nothing Then
            Set copyRange = wscf.Range("J" & i)
        Else
            Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Range("J" & i))
        End If
        If wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value <> "" Then
            offsetCount = offsetCount + 1
            copyRange.Cells(offsetCount, 2).Value = CStr(wscf.Cells(i, "J").Value) & " " & CStr(wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value)
        End If
    End If
Next i
```

In this updated code, I have used the `CStr` function to convert the range values to strings before concatenating them.

Please give it a try, and let me know if it resolves the issue and produces the desired outcome.
Problem has not been solved

Apologies for the inconvenience. I apologize for the confusion caused by the previous responses.

To correctly concatenate the values from columns J and H, we need to treat them as separate range objects and concatenate their values.

Please try the following modified code:

```VBA
Dim copyRange As Range
Dim i As Long
Dim lastRow As Long
Dim offsetCount As Long

lastRow = wscf.Cells(wscf.Rows.Count, "I").End(xlUp).Row
offsetCount = 0

For i = 5 To lastRow
    If wscf.Cells(i, "I").Value = "" Then
        If copyRange Is Nothing Then
            Set copyRange = wscf.Cells(i, "J")
        Else
            Set copyRange = Union(copyRange, wscf.Cells(i, "J"))
        End If
        If wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value <> "" Then
            offsetCount = offsetCount + 1
            copyRange.Cells(offsetCount).Offset(0, 1).Value = wscf.Cells(i, "J").Value & " " & wscf.Cells(i, "H").Value
        End If
    End If
Next i
```

In this updated code, I have adjusted the logic to concatenate the values correctly. The `copyRange` is set for column J, and we concatenate the values of column J and H using the `&` operator and assign it to the appropriate offset cell in column K.

Please give it a try, and let me know if it resolves the issue and produces the desired outcome.
